title: Forcing a Password Reset at Next Login
description:
## Make One User Reset Their Password

A single user's password may have been compromised. You want to force **that user** to set a new password the **next time they log in**, without disrupting anyone else. What do you do in Keycloak?

options.text: Set the **Update Password** required action on that user, so they must reset it at next login.
**Delete the user** and ask them to register again.
Change the **realm password policy** for everyone in the realm.
Lengthen the SSO session so the old password is used less often.

explanation: **Required actions** are tasks a user must complete at their next login (verify email, configure OTP, update profile, update password). To force one user to reset their password, add the **Update Password** required action on that user — they'll be prompted to set a new password before getting a session, and nobody else is affected. Option **b** is heavy-handed and loses the account's profile, roles, and history. Option **c** changes the policy for the **entire realm**, which is broader than needed and still wouldn't force *this* user to reset right now. Option **d** is irrelevant to forcing a password change.

hints.title: Per-user, at next login
hints.body: Required actions live on the individual user (or as realm defaults) and fire on the next authentication — exactly the surgical tool for one account.
